title: Intern Engineering
description:
Job Description: Engineering Intern (Manufacturing)

Location: Tenneco – Blacksburg, Virginia
Department: Engineering / Operations

Position Summary
The Engineering Intern will support the Blacksburg facility by assisting with process improvements, standardization, and daily engineering activities in a high-volume, unionized manufacturing environment. This role provides hands-on experience in production processes, data analysis, and continuous improvement, with a strong focus on developing and sustaining Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).

Key Responsibilities
- Assist in developing, updating, and standardizing Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for production lines (Auto Lines, IROX, Material Prep)
- Support process improvement initiatives to increase efficiency, reduce waste, and improve quality
- Collect and analyze production data to identify trends and performance gaps
- Participate in root cause analysis (5-Why, Fishbone, 8D support)
- Assist with continuous improvement (CI) projects, including 5S, layout improvements, and workflow optimization
- Provide daily production support, including troubleshooting and problem-solving with supervisors and engineers
- Observe and document processes to ensure compliance with standard work
- Support implementation of lean manufacturing principles
- Collaborate with Production, Quality, Maintenance, Supply Chain, and EHS teams
- Participate in and support safety initiatives, ensuring adherence to plant safety policies

Qualifications
- Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical, Industrial, Manufacturing Engineering, or related field
- Completed sophomore year preferred
- Strong interest in manufacturing and process improvement
- Basic knowledge of lean manufacturing and problem-solving tools preferred
- Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint
- Strong analytical and organizational skills
- Effective communication and teamwork abilities

Key Competencies
- Attention to detail
- Problem-solving mindset
- Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
- Initiative and accountability
- Strong interpersonal skills

Working Conditions
- Manufacturing plant environment with regular time spent on the production floor
- Exposure to noise, moving equipment, and varying temperatures
- Required use of personal protective equipment (PPE), including safety glasses and steel-toe shoes

Physical Requirements
- Ability to stand and walk for extended periods
- Ability to occasionally lift up to 25 pounds
- Ability to bend, stoop, climb stairs, and reach as needed
- Frequent use of a computer and standard office equipment

Schedule & Compensation
- Flexible schedule to accommodate academic commitments
- Typically 10–20 hours per week during the school term
- Opportunity for increased hours during breaks or summer
- Competitive hourly rate based on academic level

Learning Opportunities
- Hands-on experience in a high-volume automotive manufacturing environment
- Exposure to lean manufacturing, CI tools, and real-time problem solving
- Opportunity to work alongside experienced engineers and plant leadership

title: Manufacturing Engineer
description:
BASIC FUNCTION: Contribute to a high level of equipment uptime through implementation of TPM with predictive and preventive and planned maintenance programs. Foster continual improvement in equipment reliability through involvement with planning, installation, modification and repair of equipment.

SPECIFIC RESPONSIBILITIES:
1.    Plan and implement maintenance projects.  Ensure that parts are available prior to beginning the job, that all procedures and implementation of projects are understood by all personnel involved, and/or direct the implementation of the project as required.
2.    Develop and utilize procedure, maintenance schedules, preventive/predictive maintenance programs, parts crib inventories, quality control programs, PAQ-91 documentation, etc.in line with TPM guidelines and principles, to achieve a high level of machine uptime.
3.    Maintain constant alert for hazardous conditions or practices and initiate corrective action as required.
4.    Improve profitability by initiating and implementing cost reductions.
5.    Improve the reliability of equipment by implementing, predictive/preventive maintenance programs, or redesigning/modifying as necessary.
6.    Implement and manage fully integrated TPM system
7.    Coach and train operators, team leaders, supervision and other engineers in application of TPM tools
8.    Assist in the development, coordination, and installation of capital projects.
9.    Assist in complying with all  health, safety, and environmental regulations.
10.  Perform other duties as assigned.

EDUCATION:

B.S. Degree in mechanical engineering, electrical engineering, industrial technology, or plant engineering.  Equivalent experience may be substituted for the specified education requirement.

title: Sales Manager, Commercial Truck & Off Highway
description:
At Tenneco, we don’t follow industry standards; we set them, and we don’t settle for being best-in-class because we hustle to be better than best-in-class. Whether it’s our Core Values – radical candor, simplify, organizational velocity, tenacious execution and win – or our Get Stuff Done (GSD) mindset, we’re determined to become the most trusted partner and best manufacturer and distributor to the transportation industry.

 

 

With a product portfolio as expansive as it is innovative, an obsessive commitment to quality and excellence, and a global presence, we’re all about getting stuff done, so we can win.

 

 

How do we make it happen? Through the Tenneco Way. Fueled by our Core Values, a winning mindset and a relentless commitment to excellence, the Tenneco Way is how we win. It’s what keeps Team Tenneco bold, driven, and unapologetically focused on pushing past limits and redefining success.

 

 

Here, you’ll work alongside a team of relentless problem-solvers who are committed to making a tangible impact. If you’re ready to break boundaries, deliver results, and enjoy the ride along the way, you’ll thrive here.

 

 

Want to learn more about who we are? Check out our website to discover the Tenneco Way

 

 

Job Description:

 

The Sales Manager, Commercial Truck & Off Highway, will act as a liaison between customers and Tenneco Engineering, Manufacturing and Finance teams and will be the main Tenneco representative, interacting with the Purchasing, Marketing, Engineering, and Quality functions at each customer.

 

This is an individual contributor role and will not have any direct reports.

 

This position is responsible for developing and managing current business commercial issues and new business opportunities for Tenneco’s Powertrain Americas business unit.

 

Positiion Requirements & Responsibilites:

 

Responsible for supporting one or multiple customers.
Liaise primarily with the Sales Director, Sales management and other Sales Managers, Application Engineering and Operations.
Works across all functional groups of the company and other Tenneco locations worldwide.
Act as key customer contact and be responsible for customer portals within the Tenneco organization (one face to the customer) to ensure customer requirements are met.
Evaluate and keep record of Tenneco’s competitive position and market share for every customer on a continuous basis, liaise closely with the Sales Director.
Share relevant information with the Sales Department and other functional groups of the company as well as with other Tenneco plants, as may be deemed necessary and appropriate.
Visit customers on a regular basis and support both the Sales Department and Application Engineering in developing specific sales strategies for customers, projects and parts.
Contribute actively to establishing annual sales plans and strategic plans as well as medium- and long-term sales forecasts by customer, as may be required by the Sales Department.
Support special projects and corporate activities such as material recovery, red ink recovery, SOPS, APO and profit enhancement.
Identify and propose new business opportunities for Tenneco in close collaboration with Application Engineering.
Gather information relevant to prices and supply agreements, initiate cost calculations with the plants and support the Sales Department in providing proposals for pricing and contracts, both for new and existing business.
Start and maintain RFQ process in collaboration with Sales Administration.
In close collaboration with Sales Administration, prepare and submit quotation with possible documentation to customer.
Support contract review and maintain files in the local drive-in close collaboration with Application Engineering.
Internal distribution of customer specific requirements and specifications in close collaboration with Sales Administration.
Receive requests for quotation from customers, share them with Sales Administration, provide corresponding quotations to the customers as may be appropriate, and negotiate or assist the Sales Manager in negotiating high level contracts and / or supply agreements with customers.
Follow up on current quotations with customers at regular intervals or as may be required and inform the Sales Department and Application Engineering on the progress of the corresponding engine programs.
Support the Sales Director, as may be required, in evaluating and clarifying contractual matters and other issues of particular importance with customers. Ensure best possible profitability of all OE and OES parts delivered to customers. Help analyze margins, both for existing and new business, and identify anomalies or opportunities for targeted improvements.
Have an understanding on competitors’ prices and pricing strategies, evaluate this information (“benchmarking”) whenever appropriate, and keep the Sales Director duly updated on the results.
Identify and monitor the long-term development trends marked out by customers as well as competitors’ strategies and market activities, and keep the Sales Department, R & D and Application Engineering informed.
Liaise with and support the Tenneco Aftermarket Organization, as may be required from time to time.
Support new acquisitions and joint venture activities of the Tenneco Corporation, as may be requested by the management.
Review sales plans and forecasts as well as the status of customer programs, report variances and initiate or propose corrective action, if necessary in agreement with the Sales Director and / or Application Engineering.
Lead key new business opportunity development and gate review (0-1). Drive cross functional team to develop the financials plan including capex plan to secure the opportunity.
Ensure a close and open working relationship with all relevant Tenneco disciplines, with other Account Managers and Sales Administration, with local representatives, wherever applicable, as well as with Application Engineering, Project Management, Research & Development, Logistics, Quality, Manufacturing and Finance.
Support manufacturing location decisions (MLD) and coordinate and help obtaining customer approvals for the transfer of parts to other locations.
Identify cost down opportunities (together with Application Engineering and Operations, as may be seen appropriate), liaise with the Sales Director, and maintain log of all price proposals made to customers.
Support logistics in resolving delivery problems and other critical customer issues, as may be required.
Gather regular information on new engine programs and engine build rates from customers and advise the Sales Department and Application Engineering accordingly. Make sure that these numbers are duly considered in annual sales plans and strategic forecasts.
Provide customer call reports and internal meeting minutes.
Deliver reports on new business awards, wins and losses, new business opportunities, threats, pricing and cost issues etc. to the Sales Director on a regular basis, as required.
Capable of providing support as requested by supervisor.

 

 

Education:

 

Bachelors Degree in Bussiness Administration, Enginering, Finace, Economics, or similar

 

Experience:

 

5-8 years in the Powertrain/ICE industry
Valvetrain experience is preferred
Commercial truck and off-highway experience preferred

 

 

Equal opportunity employer as to all protected groups, including protected veterans and individuals with disabilities.

title: Engineering Manager
description:
JOB TITLE:                         Engineering Services Manager

LOCATION NAME:           El Paso, TX

COUNTRY:                          USA

BUSINESS UNIT:               Systems Protection Group

REPORTS TO:                     Plant Manager

MATRIX TO:                        n/a

DIRECT REPORTS:            Manufacturing Engineers, Engineering Clerk, Process Engineer

FUNCTION:                         Operations

REVISION LEVEL:              Rev 12

REVISION DATE:               4/1/2026

 

SUMMARY: The Engineering Services Manager is responsible for leading and coordinating engineering services that support safe, efficient, and reliable manufacturing operations. This role owns plant-level engineering support functions including maintenance engineering, utilities, infrastructure, equipment reliability, and capital project execution.

Acting as the primary interface between Operations, Maintenance, Quality, EHS, and Corporate Engineering, the Engineering Services Manager ensures engineering standards are met while driving continuous improvement in equipment performance, cost, compliance, and capacity utilization.

 

RESPONSIBILITIES:

	
Lead and manage engineering services activities supporting production, maintenance, and plant infrastructure.
Provide technical expertise for troubleshooting equipment, process, and facility-related issues.
Ensure engineering solutions align with plant goals for safety, quality, delivery, and cost.
Establish and maintain engineering standards, specifications, and best practices.
Partner with Maintenance and Operations to improve equipment reliability, uptime, and performance.
Support preventive and predictive maintenance strategies to reduce downtime and unplanned failures.
Lead or support root cause analysis for equipment and system failures.
Drive continuous improvement initiatives related to reliability, energy usage, and asset lifecycle management.
Lead plant capital projects from concept through design, installation, and commissioning.
Develop project scopes, budgets, timelines, and ROI justifications.
Manage contractors, vendors, and engineering service providers.
Ensure projects are delivered on time, within budget, and in compliance with safety and quality standards.
Ensure full compliance with Environmental, Health & Safety (EHS) regulations and company policies.
Support compliance with quality management systems (e.g., ISO 9001, IATF 16949, ISO 14001).
Ensure engineering changes follow formal change management and validation processes.
Promote a strong safety culture within all engineering and project activities.
Partner closely with Operations, Quality, Supply Chain, Maintenance, and Finance to support plant objectives.
Translate strategic and operational requirements into practical engineering solutions.
Provide data-driven input to support operational decision-making and long-term planning.
Lead, develop, and coach engineering services team members.
Set clear objectives, performance expectations, and development      plans.
Allocate engineering resources effectively to meet plant priorities.
Foster a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement.

 

 




EDUCATION:

	
B.S. degree in Engineering or relative discipline required.

 




EXPERIENCE:

 

 

 

 

COMPETENCIES & QUALIFICATIONS:

 

 

 

 

ORGANIZATIONAL RESPONSIBILITIES:

 

 

 

	
5–8+ years of progressive experience in manufacturing or industrial engineering environments.
 Proven experience managing engineering teams and capital projects.
Strong background in equipment, facilities, utilities, and plant infrastructure support.
Experience working in regulated manufacturing environments preferred.

 

 

Strong technical problem-solving and analytical skills
Project management and budgeting expertise
Knowledge of lean manufacturing and continuous improvement principles
Ability to analyze data and generate actionable insights
Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Excel, PowerPoint, Project)
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Ability to work effectively across functions and at all organizational levels
Demonstrated leadership, decision-making, and organizational skills

 

 

 

Adhere to the policy and practice of refusing to tolerate or allow behavior defined by Tenneco as unsafe.
Comply with Environmental Health & Safety Rules and Regulations.  Understand and adhere to the EHS Cardinal Rules.  Must be able to understand what hazards are associated with their position and how to minimize them.
All accidents/injuries/near misses no matter how slight must be reported to any member of management, and/or Environmental Health & Safety Manager who will follow escalation process.  
 Align with Tenneco Values:
Radical Candor
Organizational Velocity
Simplification
Tenacious Execution
Win
Each employee has the authority to initiate action to prevent a violation of the Code of Conduct.
Ensures compliance with federal, state, and local regulations, including Tenneco’s policies, procedures, and practices.
Adheres and is compliant with the policies and procedures for IATF16949/AS9100 and other quality standards.

title: Application Engineer LBE Valves
description:
At Tenneco, we don’t follow industry standards; we set them, and we don’t settle for being best-in-class because we hustle to be better than best-in-class. Whether it’s our Core Values–radical candor, simplify, organizational velocity, tenacious execution and win–or our Get Stuff Done (GSD) mindset, we’re determined to become the most trusted partner and best manufacturer and distributor to the transportation industry.

 

How do we make it happen? Through the Tenneco Way. Fueled by our Core Values, a winning mindset and a relentless commitment to excellence, the Tenneco Way is how we win. It’s what keeps Team Tenneco bold, driven, and unapologetically focused on pushing past limits and redefining success.

 

Here, you’ll work alongside a team of relentless problem-solvers who are committed to making a tangible impact. If you’re ready to break boundaries, deliver results, and enjoy the ride along the way, you’ll thrive here.


Want to learn more about who we are? Check out our website to discover the Tenneco Way.

As an Application Engineer Large Bore Engine (LBE) Valves, work together with the account manager to provide the necessary service to industrial customers.  The Application Engineer Large Bore Engine (LBE) Valves is the project manager responsible for the concept, design, development, and production support of Powertrain System Components and Power Cylinder Systems.  This work includes Customer Program Development (APQP); Part Design; Verification & Validation Testing; Failure Analysis; Value Analysis/Value Engineering; Customer Interfacing and Simultaneous Engineering with the manufacturing plants.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Prepare tests requests, evaluate test results, and publish final test reports.
Process Sample Orders and Request For Quotes.
Assist in root cause analysis of EOT (End of Test) or field returned parts.
Participate in the business group meetings.
Responsible for developing new products as the designated project manager for the Customer Program Development (APQP) process, if required also with other global plants and tech centers.
Responsible to ensure that the product design is in accordance to the agreed customer requirements.
Work with manufacturing plants to assure manufacturability of designs.
Required working knowledge of Powertrain Components and Power Cylinder Systems.
Ability to evaluate the business issues and prioritize activities.
Assist/direct the education and training of less experienced Application Engineers.
Ability to take a lead role in major advanced engineering projects.
Travel as required to support the customer and to support/develop new opportunities.

 

JOB REQUIREMENTS

Bachelor’s degree in engineering required.
8–10 years of experience in engineering.
Deep expertise in valves or related engineered products required.
Proven ability to conduct DOE, DFMEA, and failure analysis testing.
Strong understanding of statistics, GD&T, Tolerance Stack Ups
Demonstrated ability to drive results in cross-functional, matrixed environments.
Excellent communication, negotiation, and presentation skills.
Estimated Travel: 30-40%

We don’t want average. We want exceptional. We want someone who’s hungry to build, unafraid to challenge, and bold enough to lead with empathy, speed, and precision. Sound like you? Let us know.

title: Ride Engineer
description:
At Tenneco, we don’t follow industry standards; we set them, and we don’t settle for being best-in-class because we hustle to be better than best-in-class. Whether it’s our Core Values – radical candor, simplify, organizational velocity, tenacious execution and win – or our Get Stuff Done (GSD) mindset, we’re determined to become the most trusted partner and best manufacturer and distributor to the transportation industry.

 

With a product portfolio as expansive as it is innovative, an obsessive commitment to quality and excellence, and a global presence, we’re all about getting stuff done, so we can win.

 

How do we make it happen? Through the Tenneco Way. Fueled by our Core Values, a winning mindset and a relentless commitment to excellence, the Tenneco Way is how we win. It’s what keeps Team Tenneco bold, driven, and unapologetically focused on pushing past limits and redefining success.

 

Here, you’ll work alongside a team of relentless problem-solvers who are committed to making a tangible impact. If you’re ready to break boundaries, deliver results, and enjoy the ride along the way, you’ll thrive here.


Want to learn more about who we are? Check out our website to discover the Tenneco Way

Ride Performance Engineering - Engineer

This person in this position will provide technical information for engineering design, development, and release of shock absorber, strut, and suspension module products for OE automotive customers. Employee will regularly communicate with customers regarding technical engineering topics, and will be responsible for hands-on damper hydraulic tuning, semi-active calibration, discussing engineering design, assisting with development activities and providing some manufacturing support to achieve successful product launches.  Responsibilities include: 

Meet with Tenneco management and customer to understand technical requirements for new business opportunities.
Assist with development and optimization of high value, cost effective new part or system designs based on customer requirements and Tenneco engineering and manufacturing capabilities.
Develop and deliver design presentations to customer that demonstrates how Tenneco can meet their needs.
Provide support to the manufacturing production facility to launch new products and resolve technical issues.
Assist with analysis of customer field returns and support warranty return analysis.
Ensure compliance with customer drawings and specifications.  Resolve requirement issues directly with customer engineers.
Support manufacturing facilities in developing PFMEA's and control plans.

Required Skills

Foundation in engineering fundamentals, especially statics, fluid dynamics, & product design.
Sound written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to write concise reports & to compose and deliver presentations.
Good computer skills in Excel & Power Point.  Experience in Canape is preferred.
Ability to read and comprehend engineering/technical documents.
Hands-on problem-solving skills, including capability to work comfortably around test and manufacturing assembly equipment.
Strong in teamwork & with goal of leadership.  Must work effectively across multiple levels of the organization to drive team success.
Flexible to travel.

Required Experience

BS in Mechanical Engineering
Experience / Knowledge in automotive product design and development

 

Equal opportunity employer as to all protected groups, including protected veterans and individuals with disabilities

title: Designer
description:
Designer

JOB SUMMARY:

Individual will be responsible to develop 3D models and engineering prints for entire exhaust systems and/or its components for any given vehicle platform. Expect close collaboration with Engineering functions to make sure design meets internal and customer requirements

 

JOB RESPONSIBILITIES:

Create complex 3D models that comply with vehicle packaging requirements and internal design guidelines
Execution of design tasks as defined in the Engineering Change Management document (ECM)
Accurately represent product tolerances and datum structure
Develop engineering prints with adequate tolerances and datum schemes
Ability to accurately build and maintain Engineering Bill of Materials
Present design proposals to Engineering community
Complete design within the specified deadline

 

 

JOB REQUIREMENTS:

EDUCATION:

Bachelor’s Degree in Mechanical Engineering or Related Field

 

REQUIREMENTS/CERTIFICATIONS:

Proficient in NX and/or CATIA and/or CREO design software for 3D and 2D modeling
Excellent communication and presentation skills
Knowledge and understanding of GD&T design rules
Basic knowledge of APQP guidelines
Manage and prioritize several tasks at once
Basic understanding of manufacturing processes (welding & metal forming)

 

 

Other Duties: Additional duties, responsibilities, and activities may be required

 

An Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, or protected veteran status and will not be discriminated against on the basis of disability.

title: Controls Engineer
description:
At Tenneco, we don’t follow industry standards; we set them, and we don’t settle for being best-in-class because we hustle to be better than best-in-class. Whether it’s our Core Values – radical candor, simplify, organizational velocity, tenacious execution and win – or our Get Stuff Done (GSD) mindset, we’re determined to become the most trusted partner and best manufacturer and distributor to the transportation industry.

 

With a product portfolio as expansive as it is innovative, an obsessive commitment to quality and excellence, and a global presence, we’re all about getting stuff done, so we can win.

 

How do we make it happen? Through the Tenneco Way. Fueled by our Core Values, a winning mindset and a relentless commitment to excellence, the Tenneco Way is how we win. It’s what keeps Team Tenneco bold, driven, and unapologetically focused on pushing past limits and redefining success.

 

Here, you’ll work alongside a team of relentless problem-solvers who are committed to making a tangible impact. If you’re ready to break boundaries, deliver results, and enjoy the ride along the way, you’ll thrive here.


Want to learn more about who we are? Check out our website to discover the Tenneco Way

Job Title: Controls Engineer

We are seeking a skilled and detail-oriented Controls Engineer to design, develop, and implement control systems for automated machinery and processes. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in electrical engineering, control theory, and programming, and will be responsible for ensuring systems operate efficiently, safely, and reliably.

Key Responsibilities:

Design and develop control systems and algorithms to meet project specifications.
Program and configure PLCs, SCADA, HMI, and other control interfaces.
Conduct system integration, testing, and debugging to ensure functionality and reliability.
Perform diagnostics and troubleshooting of control systems in real-time.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including project managers, mechanical engineers, and maintenance staff.
Create detailed documentation including specifications, schematics, and user manuals.
Ensure compliance with safety regulations and industry standards.
Stay current with technological advancements in automation and control systems.
Provide training and technical support to users and maintenance personnel.
Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives for control systems and processes.

Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Control Systems Engineering, or a related field.
2–5 years of experience in control systems design and implementation.
Proficiency in programming languages such as C++, Python, or Java.
Experience with Allen Bradley, Siemens, or similar PLC platforms.
Familiarity with AutoCAD, MATLAB, and other engineering software tools.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication and teamwork abilities.

Preferred Skills:

Experience in high-volume manufacturing or industrial automation.
Knowledge of NFPA, NEC, and other relevant electrical codes.
Certification such as EIT, PMP, or PLC programming credentials.
Experience with predictive maintenance and continuous improvement methodologies.

Equal opportunity employer as to all protected groups, including protected veterans and individuals with disabilities

title: Key Account Manager
description:
At Tenneco, we don’t follow industry standards; we set them, and we don’t settle for being best-in-class because we hustle to be better than best-in-class. Whether it’s our Core Values – radical candor, simplify, organizational velocity, tenacious execution and win – or our Get Stuff Done (GSD) mindset, we’re determined to become the most trusted partner and best manufacturer and distributor to the transportation industry.

 

 

With a product portfolio as expansive as it is innovative, an obsessive commitment to quality and excellence, and a global presence, we’re all about getting stuff done, so we can win.

 

 

How do we make it happen? Through the Tenneco Way. Fueled by our Core Values, a winning mindset and a relentless commitment to excellence, the Tenneco Way is how we win. It’s what keeps Team Tenneco bold, driven, and unapologetically focused on pushing past limits and redefining success.

 

 

Here, you’ll work alongside a team of relentless problem-solvers who are committed to making a tangible impact. If you’re ready to break boundaries, deliver results, and enjoy the ride along the way, you’ll thrive here.


Want to learn more about who we are? Check out our website to discover the Tenneco Way

 

 


 




EDUCATION:

	

Degree in Engineering, Degree in Finance or Economics, Bachelor of Business Administration, or similar

 




EXPERIENCE:

	Preferred 5-8 years in Automotive/Light Vehicles industry


 

POSITION SUMMARY: 

	

 

The Key Account Manager serves as the primary interface between Tenneco and strategic automotive customers. This role drives customer satisfaction, business growth, and operational alignment by managing  relationships & new business opportunities, supporting program execution, and collaborating across departments within Tenneco.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:	
Act as the single point of contact for assigned OEM automotive customers.
Serve as key customer contact. Manage customer portals and ensure timely, accurate communication of customer requirements and updates.
Conduct regular customer visits to streng then relationships and support program development. Partner with Application Engineering in developing sales strategies for these programs.
Work closely with Sales, Application Engineering, Project Management, Manufacturing, Logistics, Research & Development, and Quality to ensure alignment with customer expectations.
Support Manufacturing Location Decisions (MLD) and coordinate customer approvals for the transfer of parts to other locations.
Contribute to annual sales plans, strategic growth initiatives, and both medium and long-term sales forecasts.
Monitor and advise the Sales Department and Application Engineering of customer build schedules, new engine programs, and market trends. Ensure these numbers are included in annual sales plans and strategic forecasts.
Identify new business opportunities and collaborate with Application Engineering to develop tailored solutions.
Facilitate pricing strategy development, cost analysis, and quotation preparation in coordination with Sales Support.
Support contract negotiations and maintain documentation for pricing agreements and supply terms.
Manage special projects and corporate activities such as material recovery, red ink recovery, SOPS, APO and profit enhancement.
Track competitor pricing, pricing strategies, market activities, and strategic movements. Keep the Sales Department, Research & Development, and Application Engineering informed.
Provide benchmarking insights to the Sales Director, when appropriate, to support competitive positioning.
Assist in resolving delivery issues, coordinating manufacturing location decisions, securing customer approvals for part transfers, and other critical customer issues as required.
Identify cost reduction opportunities in collaboration with Application Engineering and Operations, as appropriate.
Deliver timely reports on customer interactions, program status, pricing updates, new business awards, threats, and business wins/losses to Sales Support.
Maintain accurate records of customer requirements, contracts, and internal communications in close collaboration with Sales Support

 

 

Equal opportunity employer as to all protected groups, including protected veterans and individuals with disabilities
